note: R60 M3 hardening of the single-writer embedded-KIRA cutover contract. v2 replaces the v1 prose/keyword inference with TYPED, EXACT machine data: kill-switch direction, canonical seam identity, the allowed transition graph, the rollback edge, crash laws, and the single-writer invariant are all enumerated as data the verifier consumes directly — no regex over prose, so negation-smuggling ('dual writes are NOT forbidden') cannot pass. Still LAW-ONLY: scripts/verify-cutover-contract.mjs is a DRY-RUN verifier over an in-memory two-store simulation. NOT a live-store integration; no SQLite/libSQL dependency exists or is introduced this round.

authored.directive: https://github.com/aumara-xyz/aukora/issues/20 — ROUND_DIRECTIVE R60, Sam 3 item 1 (M3 typed cutover hardening)

canonicality_registry.note: Every durable writer/receipt seam is named here; there is no unnamed store. Exactly ONE seam may hold role CANONICAL_DURABLE_MEMORY_WRITER at any instant. The verifier derives the canonical writer from this typed role field, not from prose.

seams.name: convex-memoryChain
seams.detail: The one canonical durable memory writer today. Idempotent by content-addressed recordId (R34), no-resurrection (R44), receipt-before-row. Canonical until a cutover checkpoint under this contract completes.

seams.name: in-process-door-receipts
seams.detail: In-process door receipt store. A receipt lane, never a canonical memory writer; DurableReceiptBridge stays inert until its own governed brick.

seams.name: trusted-state-store
seams.detail: Crash-safe authority-consumption journal (consumed-ID / PREPARED state), primary-wired on the live ceremony path (apps/seed/src/durableCandidateMonitor.ts). Durable, so it is NAMED here — but it journals authority state, not memory rows; it is NOT a durable memory writer and never becomes canonical for memory. Its cross-process writer-lock + atomic-append discipline is the model the embedded prerequisite below reuses.

seams.name: embedded-kira-store
seams.detail: The embedded KIRA store (SQLite/libSQL class). DOES NOT EXIST this round. May only enter as a shadow follower and may only become canonical through the phase machine below. A live embedded writer outside this contract is forbidden (R53 law: do not add a fourth live store).

kill_switch.semantics: When armed, cutover progress is REFUSED at every gate; if already EMBEDDED_CANONICAL the only permitted move is rollback to the checkpoint boundary. direction is TYPED (enum), so 'fail forward' cannot be smuggled in as prose — the verifier asserts direction === FAIL_SAFE_TOWARD_CONVEX.

dual_writes.detail: Typed boolean, not a prose 'FORBIDDEN' substring. The Convex→embedded transition uses shadow reads with parity measurement; the follower accepts no direct writes while following. A tamper setting allowed:true (or any negation-smuggled prose) fails the verifier.

ordering.detail: Preserved unchanged from the organism law: no record chains without passing the immune/content scan first; every durable effect commits its receipt before the effect row is visible.

writer_lock_prerequisite.note: EXECUTABLE prerequisite (answers Auma's documented current embedded-path race without pretending the store exists). The embedded follower cannot reach PARITY_PROVEN or CUTOVER_CHECKPOINT unless a cross-process writer lock + atomic-append capability is acquired and held. Modeled on trusted-state-store's discipline.

checkpoint.note: ONE atomic artifact carrying every continuity anchor. A checkpoint missing any required field group MUST refuse cutover. v2 extends v1 with the erase-authority and writer-epoch continuity the R60 audit (M3) found missing.

field_semantics.signed_heads_high_water: signedHeads high-water at cutover; embedded may never accept a head below it (rollback-attack fence).
field_semantics.record_id_derivation: content-addressed recordId derivation rule identity (R34), pinned so both stores derive identical IDs — the parity comparator's join key.
field_semantics.receipt_chain_anchors: receipt-chain anchor digests at cutover; the embedded receipt continuation chains from the exact Convex-era head.
field_semantics.no_resurrection_tombstone_set: tombstone set digest AND explicit no-resurrection continuity marker; erased records stay erased in the embedded store (R44 carries over, not merely re-derived).
field_semantics.erase_attestations_consumed_nonces: consumed erase-attestation nonce set digest; consumed nonces stay consumed across cutover (no replay window opens on the new writer).
field_semantics.erase_root_registry: digest + version of the registered erase-authority owner-root registry (Sam 2 M1); the embedded store inherits the exact root binding so cross-root erase cannot appear post-cutover.
field_semantics.writer_epoch_fence: the monotonic writer-epoch/fence value; the pre-cutover epoch is fenced off so no stale Convex-epoch write is accepted after the flip (single-writer continuity across the boundary).

phases.forbidden_edges_note: Any edge not in transition_graph is forbidden. In particular there is NO SHADOW_READS→EMBEDDED_CANONICAL or PARITY_PROVEN→EMBEDDED_CANONICAL edge that skips CUTOVER_CHECKPOINT, and no forward edge out of EMBEDDED_CANONICAL. The verifier reconstructs the legal graph from this typed list and rejects added/deleted edges.

rollback_boundary: Rollback restores Convex-canonical exactly at the recorded cutover checkpoint. Embedded-side writes accepted after the checkpoint move to a quarantined reconcile set — surfaced for governed re-application, never silently dropped and never silently merged.
out_of_scope_this_round: Any SQLite/libSQL dependency or embedded store implementation
Any provider call, Nebius arming, or Cell 0A interaction
DurableReceiptBridge wiring (its own future governed brick)
